summary: A low, symmetrical temple-like single building with a cruciform plan and stepped dome/roof at the center. The structure sits inside a shallow bordered yard with regularly spaced low cobblestone posts. Walls are layered with horizontal banding and small recessed alcoves; a long central walkway/approach aligns with the main axial entrance.

shape.overall_form: cruciform (cross-shaped) plan with central raised dome and four projecting arms; surrounded by a rectangular yard
shape.symmetry: fourfold axial symmetry along a main longitudinal axis (approximate)

materials.name: nether_brick
materials.role: dark roof banding and dome cap

materials.name: glowstone
materials.role: small light at dome peak and entrance

elements: small recessed alcoves and horizontal banding on walls
perimeter low cobblestone posts forming a rectangular boundary
glowstone light at dome apex and near entrance
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular yard with a single-block-deep border and evenly spaced cobblestone posts forming an inner rectangle.
Lay a central long rectangular approach (stone slab/stone) leading to the main entrance; align the cruciform arms symmetrically around it.

Build primary walls in stonebrick with stepped setbacks: each arm is composed of stacked horizontal layers with recessed alcoves and a repeating band (use carved/ornate stonebrick patterns if available).
Create a stepped, concentric dome/roof at the center using alternating darker slabs (nether-brick or dark slabs) and lighter slabs for the stepped look; top with glowstone.
Use smooth slabs for the walkway and roof edge caps; place glowstone at apex and small accent lights at entrance corners.
uncertainties: Exact block types: some patterns could be stonebrick variants (mossy or chiseled) rather than plain stonebrick; identified as stonebrick with 0.9 confidence.
Roof material: dark bands appear like nether brick slabs/stairs but could be dark wood or other dark block; confidence 0.6.
Interior layout and any interior floors or rooms are not visible and assumed single-floor with hollow interior.
Exact footprint dimensions are estimated from images and may be off by several blocks.
Some slab vs full-block usages (roof and walkway) are inferred and not always distinguishable from images.
